title: Site-saturation mutagenesis library cloning
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1c80p","text":"This protocol describes the creation of a site-saturation mutagenesis library for gene variant analysis. It begins with ordering site-saturation mutagenesis oligonucleotide pools from Twist Biosciences for each position in the gene of interest, excluding position 1. These pools, containing all possible synonymous and missense variants for each codon, are combined into tiled sublibraries to cover the entire gene sequence, with overlap to ensure completeness.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bjam","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5l8rt","text":"This protocol enables the generation of a comprehensive mutant library for functional analysis of the gene, providing insights into the effects of various mutations. The process involves careful handling of materials, precise PCR and assembly steps, and thorough bacterial transformation and culture techniques.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"br443","text":"Site-saturation mutagenesis oligonucleotide pools were ordered from Twist Biosciences for each position in the gene of interest, except position 1. In these oligonucleotide pools, each position contains one codon for each synonymous or missense variant. For genes longer than 164 amino acids, the libraries were tiled to encompass the desired length of the gene of interest, including 20 positions of overlap between adjacent sub libraries.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Resuspend 50 ng of each oligonucleotide pool in 10 µL nuclease-free water.
Pool oligonucleotides in equal volumes into tiled sublibraries.
Set up inverse PCR reaction as follows (for 1 30 µL reaction): %s %s
Run the inverse PCR protocol as follows: %s %s
Using a 1% agarose gel with 1X SYBR Safe, run the inverse PCR product on the gel at 100V for 00:45:00 .
Using a blue-light transilluminator and clean razor blade, cut the correct product size out of the gel and extract the product from the gel using the QIAquick Gel Extraction Kit.
Set up a Gibson assembly with the backbone PCR product and each of 3 pooled sublibraries at a 5:1 molar ratio of insert:backbone, as follows: %s %s
Run the reaction at 50 °C for 01:00:00 . Store On ice between the end of incubation and cleanup.
Clean Gibson products with Zymo clean and concentrator-5 kit, eluting in 10 µL water. Move on to electroporation.
Pre-chill electroporation cuvettes (2 replicates for each sublibrary tile, 1 positive control, 1 negative control) On ice for at least 00:30:00 .
Thaw an appropriate volume of NEB 10-beta electrocompetent cells On ice for 00:10:00 .
Pipette 25 µL NEB 10-beta electrocompetent cells into each pre-chilled cuvette. Take care not to introduce bubbles to the cuvette.

Add 1 µL of cleaned Gibson-assembled product into each of the replicate cuvettes. Add 1 µL pUC19 to the positive control and 1 µL of negative control from the Gibson assembly to the negative control cuvette. Take care not to introduce bubbles to the cuvette.
Allow the cells and plasmids to incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .

During incubation: prepare recovery tubes. For each sublibrary: add 750 µL SOC into 1 recovery tube (both electroporations will be combined) For each control: add 875 µL SOC into 1 recovery tube. Pre-warm the recovery tubes at 37 °C .
Wipe each cuvette down with a kimwipe to ensure no water or ice is stuck to the cuvette. Gently tap on the table top to ensure all liquid is collected at the bottom of the cuvette.
Electroporate each cuvette at 6 kV for 2 ms.
Recover each sample with 100 µL pre-warmed SOC, then add all 125 µL to the appropriate recovery tube – combine replicate electroporations. Total volume in the recovery tube should be 1 mL .
Recover the cells in a shaking incubator ( 225 rpm, °C, ) at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .
During incubation, prepare a growth flask for each sample. Add 49 mL LB and 50 µL of carbenicillin to each flask.
After recovery incubation, add the entire 1 mL volume to the prepared growth flask. Incubate with shaking for 00:03:00 .

After 3 minutes, take a 200 µL sample from each flask to use for estimating colony counts via serial dilutions on LB-amp plates. Return the growth flasks to the incubator and incubate with shaking ( 225 rpm, °C, ) for 16:00:00 at 37 °C . Serial dilution plates should be incubated for 16 hours at 37C in a standing incubator.
After 16 hours, remove flasks from incubator. Transfer the culture to a 50-mL falcon tube and spin for 4000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .
While the cultures are spinning, count colonies on the serial dilution plates.
Use the Qiagen Plasmid Plus Midi kit according to the manufacturer’s protocol to extract plasmids from the culture pellets.
Move on to barcoding the library plasmids.
creator: Sarah Heidl
